g.text: AVX-SSE Transition Penalty: The vzeroupper Problem

div.p: Why this happens: XMM registers are the lower 128 bits of YMM registers. When AVX code writes YMM0, the upper half becomes "dirty". If SSE code then reads XMM0, the CPU must save the dirty upper half before the SSE operation can proceed—this save/restore costs ~70 cycles on Intel Sandy Bridge through Skylake. vzeroupper clears the upper bits, avoiding the stall.
Zen 2+ and Ice Lake+ have reduced penalty. Keep vzeroupper for legacy/portable compat.
